info.title: Complaint Resolution System API
info.description: Backend API for the Maharashtra State Electricity Board Complaint Resolution System.

tags.name: Consumer Devices
tags.description: Push-notification device registration for verified consumers

tags.name: Staff Devices
tags.description: Push-notification device registration for authenticated staff users

tags.name: Consumer Master Data (read)
tags.description: Active master data for the consumer submit form

tags.name: Consumer Complaints
tags.description: Submit and read back consumer-raised complaints

tags.name: Staff Directory
tags.description: Resolve staff user ids to names + roles

tags.name: Technician Complaints
tags.description: Technician lifecycle actions: start, resolve, add resolution images, close

tags.name: Staff Complaint Management
tags.description: Engineer / Admin actions on complaints: assign, reassign, severity, reject, mark-duplicate

get.summary: Current staff profile

put.description: Updates the caller's own mutable profile fields: fullName, email, mobile, notificationsPushEnabled. Scope fields (role, subdivisionId, distributionCenterId, employeeId) are immutable here — those are admin actions via PUT /api/v1/admin/staff/{id}.

get.summary: Fetch one staff account by ID

put.summary: Update an existing staff account (profile + DC reassignment)

post.summary: Start work on an ASSIGNED complaint (→ IN_PROGRESS)
Mark an IN_PROGRESS complaint RESOLVED (SLA-breach reason required if late)
Upload up to 3 resolution images (multipart, image/jpeg or image/png)
Close a RESOLVED complaint that is assigned to the calling technician
post.description: BRD §4.8: technician is the normal closing actor. Caller must be the assigned technician; complaint must be RESOLVED. Body's slaBreachReason is required only when the complaint is SLA-breached and no reason was already captured at resolve time.

post.summary: Register (or refresh) the calling staff user's device for push
post.description: Idempotent: re-posting the same deviceId refreshes the push token in place. Returns 201 on first registration, 200 on refresh.

post.summary: Update severity of a non-terminal complaint
Reject a SUBMITTED complaint with a reason
Reassign an already-assigned complaint to a different technician
Mark a SUBMITTED complaint as a duplicate of another (by ticket number)
Engineer / Admin close-on-behalf of a RESOLVED complaint
post.description: Returns the post-close detail (status=CLOSED + bumped version) so the FE can update its cache in one round-trip without a follow-up GET.

post.summary: Assign a SUBMITTED complaint to a technician and set severity
Rotate refresh token; returns new access + refresh pair
Revoke the provided refresh token (or all sessions if body omitted)
Login with employee ID + password
Change password and receive a fresh token pair
post.description: Persists the new password, revokes every refresh token belonging to the caller (kicks all other sessions), and returns a brand-new access + refresh pair with passwordResetRequired = false in both the JWT claims and the response envelope. Callers do not need to chain /staff/auth/refresh afterwards.

post.summary: Register (or refresh) the calling consumer's device for push
post.description: Idempotent: re-posting the same deviceId refreshes the push token in place. Returns 201 on first registration, 200 on refresh.

get.summary: Paged tracking list of every complaint the verified consumer has raised
get.description: Server pins consumer_master_id = caller.consumerMasterId(). Optional ?status=… filter. Default sort is createdAt,desc (latest first).

post.summary: Submit a new complaint with up to 3 images in a single multipart request
post.description: Multipart contract: one JSON part named `complaint` (SubmitComplaintRequest) plus 0..3 image parts named `images` (image/jpeg or image/png, each ≤ 1 MB). Ticket number, SLA deadline and signed image URLs are returned in one response — no follow-up calls required for the confirmation screen.

get.summary: Read-back the verified consumer's own feedback for a complaint (Stage 20.1)
get.description: Returns 200 with data=null when no feedback has been submitted yet. Lets the FE render a read-only rating panel without try / catching the submit 409 to discover existence. Owner-checked; foreign ticket → 403.

post.summary: Submit feedback for a CLOSED complaint (one-shot, idempotent at DB level)
post.description: Rating is 1..5; comment is optional. Allowed only after the complaint is CLOSED → 409 FEEDBACK_NOT_ALLOWED_YET while it's still open. One row per complaint (UNIQUE on complaint_id) → 409 FEEDBACK_ALREADY_SUBMITTED on a second attempt.

post.summary: Cancel an owned complaint (only while status = SUBMITTED)
post.description: Consumer-driven withdrawal. Once an engineer has assigned the complaint, MSEB owns the workflow and the consumer can no longer cancel — that's a 409 COMPLAINT_NOT_IN_SUBMITTED_STATE. Body carries an optional free-text reason.

post.summary: Verify an OTP and receive a 5-minute consumer verification token
post.description: On success, returns a non-refreshable 5-minute JWT carrying consumerId, consumerMasterId, and the OTP-verified mobile. Use it on every /api/v1/consumer/** call. After expiry, repeat the send/verify cycle.

post.summary: Send a 6-digit OTP to the supplied mobile
post.description: Validates the Consumer ID against consumer_master, then issues a BCrypt-hashed OTP (raw never persisted or logged). Enforces a 30-second per-mobile cooldown and a max 5 sends per mobile per hour.

get.summary: List staff in the admin's subdivision (paged, filterable)

post.summary: Create a staff account; returns the one-time temporary password
Reset a staff account password; returns the one-time temporary password
Deactivate a staff account (revokes all live sessions)
Activate a staff account
Create a new subdivision
Deactivate a subdivision
Activate a subdivision
Create a DC under the admin's subdivision
Deactivate a DC
Activate a DC
Create a complaint category
Deactivate a complaint category
Activate a complaint category

get.summary: Paged list of complaints assigned to the calling technician
get.description: Server pins assigned_technician_id = caller.userId(). Optional filters: status, severity, slaBreached, dateFrom/dateTo, q.

get.summary: Batch-resolve up to 50 staff ids in one round-trip
get.description: Unknown ids are silently dropped. Order of the response is not guaranteed.

get.summary: Resolve a single staff id to a directory entry
List subdivisions (paginated)
Fetch a subdivision by id
List distribution centers (optionally filtered by subdivision)
Fetch a DC by id
List complaint categories
Fetch a category by id
Paged complaint search for engineer / admin
get.description: Filters compose with caller-scope: engineer = own DC, admin = own subdivision. Default sort is createdAt,desc.

get.summary: Engineer / Admin detail view of a complaint (scope-checked)
Status-change audit trail for a complaint (chronological)
List active complaint categories for the consumer submit dropdown
get.description: Returns only categories where active = true. Inactive rows are never surfaced to consumers.

get.summary: Fetch the verified consumer's own complaint by ticket number
get.description: Owner-checked. Stage 17 enriched the payload with severity, slaBreached, resolvedAt, closedAt. Staff identities and internal reason fields remain on the staff-side DTO only.

get.summary: Consumer-safe status-change history for an owned complaint
get.description: Same chronological ordering as the staff endpoint, but without changedByUserId — consumers don't see staff IDs.

delete.summary: Revoke the calling staff user's device (soft delete, idempotent)
delete.description: Flips active=false. Missing or already-inactive device → 204 no-op. Called by the FE logout reducer before clearing the JWT.

delete.summary: Revoke the calling consumer's device (soft delete, idempotent)
delete.description: Flips active=false. Missing or already-inactive device → 204 no-op.

imageType.description: Source of the image: consumer-submitted vs technician-uploaded at resolution.

deviceId.description: FE-generated UUID, stable per physical device

pushToken.description: Raw FCM / APNs / web push token; never logged or returned

appVersion.description: Optional app version, useful for staged rollouts

updatedAt.description: DB-managed updated_at (IST) — bumps on every refresh

DeviceTokenResponse.description: Device-token row as returned by register / refresh. pushToken is never echoed.

feedbackSubmitted.description: True iff feedback has been submitted for this complaint. Lets the tracking list render a Rated / Awaiting feedback hint without a per-row probe.
True iff the consumer has already submitted feedback for this complaint. Lets the FE hide the Rate button on first paint without a probe POST.

bearerAuth.description: Staff access token issued by POST /api/v1/staff/auth/login
